title: Add 'Add to Home Screen' button for PWA installation

requirements.text: Add a Web App Manifest file (manifest.json) in the frontend/public directory with the app name 'MediFind', short name, theme color matching the existing soft green palette, background color, display mode set to 'standalone', and appropriate icon references so the browser recognizes the app as installable.

quotes: Put add to home screen button

acceptanceCriteria: A manifest.json file exists in frontend/public with name, short_name, theme_color, background_color, display: standalone, and icons fields
The manifest is linked in frontend/index.html via a <link rel="manifest"> tag
The app theme-color meta tag is set to match the soft green palette

requirements.text: Add a visible 'Add to Home Screen' button in the app UI (e.g., in the Layout header or as a dismissible banner below the header) that appears only when the browser fires the 'beforeinstallprompt' event. When clicked, it should trigger the native browser install prompt. Once the user accepts or dismisses, hide the button. On iOS Safari (where beforeinstallprompt is not available), show a small instructional tooltip or banner explaining how to use the Share > Add to Home Screen option.

quotes: Put add to home screen button

acceptanceCriteria: An 'Add to Home Screen' button or banner is rendered in the UI when the browser supports the install prompt
Clicking the button triggers the native browser PWA install prompt
The button/banner is hidden after the prompt is accepted or dismissed
On iOS Safari, a fallback instructional message is shown instead (e.g., 'Tap the Share icon then Add to Home Screen')
The button/banner uses the existing soft green color palette and matches the app's visual theme

requirements.text: Register a minimal service worker (sw.js) in frontend/public that caches the app shell on install so the PWA passes basic installability requirements in modern browsers (Chrome, Edge). The service worker should at minimum handle the install event and cache the root URL.

quotes: Put add to home screen button

acceptanceCriteria: A sw.js service worker file exists in frontend/public
The service worker is registered in frontend/src/main.tsx or frontend/index.html
The app passes Chrome's PWA installability criteria (manifest + service worker)
The service worker does not break existing app functionality

constraints: Do not modify frontend/src/main.tsx beyond adding service worker registration if strictly necessary — prefer registering in index.html or a separate init file
Do not change backend logic or data models
The Add to Home Screen UI must match the existing soft green and white color palette
nonGoals: Publishing the app to Google Play Store or Apple App Store
Full offline support or aggressive caching beyond basic installability

required: App icon for PWA manifest, flat soft green medical cross or leaf on white background (medifind-icon.dim_192x192.png)
App icon for PWA manifest large size, flat soft green medical cross or leaf on white background (medifind-icon.dim_512x512.png)
